1497855 1434736       02:32 Sl   /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -C /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b85090ae-20260606T222020-50186326 -m gpt-5.4-mini <execute-bead>   <instructions> You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-b85090ae">     <title>Reject ancestor dependency edges in bead create and dep add</title>     <description> PROBLEM
`ddx bead dep add` and the bead create path still allow dependency edges that point back into the parent chain. That lets a child re-introduce its parent as a dep and creates the deadlock described in the parent bead.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go:520-680` validates existence, self-deps, and cycles, but it does not reject an edge when the target bead is the caller's parent or any ancestor in the parent chain. `cli/cmd/bead.go:328-378` also lacks a `--depends-on` create input path, so create-time validation has to cover both the CLI flag and the shared store guard.

PROPOSED FIX
Add ancestry-aware validation in the bead store and thread a `--depends-on` create flag through `cli/cmd/bead.go` so `ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y` hard-rejects when `X == Y` or `X` is any ancestor of `Y`. The error shape must name the offending ancestor and print the parent chain.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the decomposition path, doctor repair logic, work-loop remediation, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/store_test.go
- cli/cmd/bead.go
- cli/cmd/bead_test.go (new or existing create-path test coverage)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBeadDepAddRejectsParentAncestor` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor` passes.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passes.
4. `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, triage:no-changes-unjustified</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ff0f5dff5f0a7ad13e3de5fc134a1b7562a93b14" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260606T222020-50186326"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>   </governing> </execute-bead>

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-b85090ae">     <title>Reject ancestor dependency edges in bead create and dep add</title>     <description> PROBLEM
`ddx bead dep add` and the bead create path still allow dependency edges that point back into the parent chain. That lets a child re-introduce its parent as a dep and creates the deadlock described in the parent bead.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go:520-680` validates existence, self-deps, and cycles, but it does not reject an edge when the target bead is the caller's parent or any ancestor in the parent chain. `cli/cmd/bead.go:328-378` also lacks a `--depends-on` create input path, so create-time validation has to cover both the CLI flag and the shared store guard.

PROPOSED FIX
Add ancestry-aware validation in the bead store and thread a `--depends-on` create flag through `cli/cmd/bead.go` so `ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y` hard-rejects when `X == Y` or `X` is any ancestor of `Y`. The error shape must name the offending ancestor and print the parent chain.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the decomposition path, doctor repair logic, work-loop remediation, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/store_test.go
- cli/cmd/bead.go
- cli/cmd/bead_test.go (new or existing create-path test coverage)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBeadDepAddRejectsParentAncestor` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor` passes.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passes.
4. `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, triage:no-changes-unjustified</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ff0f5dff5f0a7ad13e3de5fc134a1b7562a93b14" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260606T222020-50186326"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. `ddx bead doctor` can currently repair oversized fields and detect dangling-success beads, but it never scans for dependency edges that point back to a bead's parent chain. That leaves structural back-edges hidden until work loops wedge.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/doctor.go` only reports field-length overflow, and `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` only exposes the oversized-field and dangling-success modes. There is no stable, scriptable `parent_ancestor_in_deps` pass that enumerates offending beads or repairs them under `--fix`.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the bead doctor to scan for any dep whose target is the bead's parent or ancestor, emit a stable `parent_ancestor_in_deps` finding list, and remove only the offending edges when `--fix` is supplied. The command output must stay deterministic so shell tooling can parse it. Add a regression test named `ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change create-time validation, decomposition prompt text, work-loop remediation, or dispatch severity in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor_test.go
- cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go
- cli/cmd/bead_doctor_test.go (new or existing command-level coverage)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBeadDoctorDetectsAndRepairsBackEdge` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadDoctorDetectsAndRepairsBackEdge` passes.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passes.
4. `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="e8924d448ec2be54596ba4b472a5c1285ca5d906" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260606T222742-3d9c568d"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved.   1196	func TestBeadDepAddRejectsParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {

     9	)
    10	
    11	func TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {
    12		workingDir := t.TempDir()
    13		factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
    14		rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
    15	
    16		rootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Root", "--type", "task")
    17		require.NoError(t, err)
    18		rootID := strings.TrimSpace(rootOut)
    19		require.NotEmpty(t, rootID)
    20	
    21		parentOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Parent", "--type", "task", "--parent", rootID)
    22		require.NoError(t, err)
    23		parentID := strings.TrimSpace(parentOut)
    24		require.NotEmpty(t, parentID)
    25	
    26		t.Run("direct-parent", func(t *testing.T) {
    27			_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child direct", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", parentID)
    28			require.Error(t, err)
    29			assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
    30			assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID)
    31			assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
    32		})
    33	
    34		t.Run("grandparent-ancestor", func(t *testing.T) {
    35			_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child ancestor", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", rootID)
    36			require.Error(t, err)
    37			assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
    38			assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), rootID)
    39			assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
    40		})
    41	}

data.text: I’ve got the main evidence already. I’m grabbing one last line reference for the helper that formats the ancestor-chain error so the report can point to the exact error shape instead of just the call site.

The requested behavior was already present in this revision, so I didn’t need to edit or commit anything.

Evidence:
- Shared store rejection is implemented in [cli/internal/bead/store.go](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b85090ae-20260606T222020-50186326/cli/internal/bead/store.go#L3030) and used during create in [cli/internal/bead/store.go](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b85090ae-20260606T222020-50186326/cli/internal/bead/store.go#L517):
  - `beadParentChain(...)` builds the parent chain.
  - `rejectAncestorDependency(...)` emits the ancestor-chain error and names the offending dependency.
- CLI create already threads `--depends-on` in [cli/cmd/bead.go](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b85090ae-20260606T222020-50186326/cli/cmd/bead.go#L358) and registers the flag at [cli/cmd/bead.go](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b85090ae-20260606T222020-50186326/cli/cmd/bead.go#L425).
- Regression coverage already exists in [cli/internal/bead/store_test.go](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b85090ae-20260606T222020-50186326/cli/internal/bead/store_test.go#L1196) and [cli/cmd/bead_create_dep_test.go](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b85090ae-20260606T222020-50186326/cli/cmd/bead_create_dep_test.go#L11).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBeadDepAddRejectsParentAncestor` passed
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor` passed
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passed
- `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passed

The worktree stayed clean throughout.
